title: Listening - Part 4: Collecting Vintage Items
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about their passion for collecting vintage or second-hand items. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I collect vintage watches. [21]I think it started because I was tired of the disposable nature of modern gadgets that break after a year[/21]. I wanted something that was built to last several lifetimes. It’s an expensive hobby, but [26]what I really value is the deep technical knowledge I’ve had to acquire about mechanical movements[/26]. I’ve basically taught myself how to do basic repairs, which is incredibly satisfying and keeps my mind sharp. || Speaker 2: For me, collecting vinyl records was [22]originally a way to reconnect with the music my parents used to play when I was a kid[/22]. It was pure nostalgia. My collection is huge now. But the most rewarding part has been [27]the sense of community I’ve found at independent record stores[/27]. I’ve met people from all walks of life who share the same obsession, and some of them have become my closest friends. || Speaker 3: I collect mid-century furniture. [23]I was initially looking for a way to furnish my first apartment on a very tight budget[/23], and I realised second-hand was much better quality than cheap flat-pack stuff. It’s turned into a passion. [28]What I love most is the thrill of the hunt[/28]. There’s nothing quite like spending a Sunday at a flea market and finding a rare designer chair hidden under a pile of junk for ten pounds. || Speaker 4: I’m into vintage fashion. [24]I started because I wanted to develop a personal style that was completely unique and didn't look like I’d just stepped out of a high-street mall[/24]. I hate following trends. Surprisingly, [29]it’s made me much more conscious of the environmental impact of the textile industry[/29]. By reusing old clothes, I feel like I’m making a small but meaningful stand against fast fashion. || Speaker 5: I collect old maps. [23]I’ve always been fascinated by how our understanding of geography has evolved over the centuries[/23]. I use them for my research as a historian. What I’ve found most beneficial, though, is [30]how much they’ve taught me about the importance of patience[/30]. It can take years to track down a specific map from a particular printer, and you learn to appreciate the long game instead of needing instant gratification.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker started their collection.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 was tired of the 'disposable nature' of modern gadgets and wanted durability.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to 'reconnect' with the music their parents played.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 was trying to furnish an apartment 'on a very tight budget'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted a style that was 'completely unique' and not from a mall.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 is fascinated by how 'geography has evolved over the centuries'.

options.A: to study the evolution of a discipline
options.E: to find alternatives to short-lived products
options.F: to save money on essential purchases
options.G: to stand out from the crowd

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has gained most from the experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 values the 'deep technical knowledge' they acquired about repairs.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values the 'sense of community' and friends made at record stores.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 loves the 'thrill of the hunt' and finding hidden gems.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 became 'much more conscious of the environmental impact'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 learned the 'importance of patience' and playing the long game.

options.A: an increased awareness of ethical issues
options.B: a potential source of future income
options.C: the excitement of the search process
options.D: a more disciplined approach to life
options.G: a sense of pride in their home
